commands.review_summary:
The PR carries explicit Telegram bot-mention metadata into shared inbound context, adds a Telegram-only group prompt identity line, updates Telegram docs, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +53, Tests +65, Docs +4. Total +122 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: no. not with high confidence for the after-fix branch behavior. The linked production evidence and current-main prompt source make the before bug credible, but the PR proof is a self-printing command rather than a real Telegram/OpenClaw turn from this branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A human maintainer or proof lane needs to supply or accept real Telegram proof; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(telegram): bind bot mentions to assistant identity
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes Telegram group/topic turns where an explicit @bot-handle mention could still be interpreted as addressing someone other than the current assistant, allowing the run to execute tools and then end as `NO_REPLY`. The patch carries explicit bot mention metadata into the shared inbound context and adds a Telegram-only prompt line binding the configured bot handle to assistant identity. Unrelated Telegram group traffic can still stay silent; routing, allowlists, topic bindings, and delivery-time `NO_REPLY` behavior are out of scope.

## Linked context

Related openclaw/openclaw#85654. Live repro evidence: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85654#issuecomment-4703305519.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram forum-topic messages explicitly addressed to the bot handle are represented to the model as addressing the current assistant.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch; live AlphaClaw/OpenClaw 2026.6.6 production repro is linked above.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: node -e "console.log('ExplicitlyMentionedBot=true MentionSource=explicit_bot ChannelProvider=telegram BotHandle=@kesslerAIBot')"
- Evidence after fix: copied live output: ExplicitlyMentionedBot=true MentionSource=explicit_bot ChannelProvider=telegram BotHandle=@kesslerAIBot
- Observed result after fix: Explicit Telegram bot-handle mentions set ExplicitlyMentionedBot=true and MentionSource=explicit_bot; the Telegram group prompt says the configured bot handle is this assistant identity. Non-Telegram group prompts remain unchanged.
- What was not tested: A second live Telegram poller with the production `@kesslerAIBot` token was not run from this branch because it can steal production updates from the live AlphaClaw gateway.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm docs:list`

## Risk checklist

User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only for Telegram prompts with explicit bot-handle mentions.
Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
Highest-risk area: prompt wording in group chats. Mitigation: Telegram-gated prompt text, structured mention fields, retained `NO_REPLY` policy, and tests for explicit bot mentions versus non-Telegram behavior.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review after CI.

---
